rounds_summary.attempted_route: sklearn MLPRegressor (256,128,64), 63-dim features: 20-dim AA composition + Tmwt scalar + sequence length + wt/mut one-hot encodings. StandardScaler, Adam, early stopping.
rounds_summary.observation: Exit 0, 147 rows saved. MAE=67.15°C — far above naive baseline for a target with typical range ±20°C, indicating near-zero predictive signal.

rounds_summary.attempted_route: Deeper MLPRegressor (512,256,128,64), 78-dim features: added 5 physicochemical props per residue (hydrophobicity, charge, MW, vdW volume, polarity), their mut-wt diffs, and relative mutation position.
rounds_summary.observation: Exit 0, 147 rows saved. MAE=64.32°C — marginal 4% improvement despite substantially richer features; MLP still predicts near-constant output.

rounds_summary.attempted_route: GradientBoostingRegressor+RandomForest ensemble (0.6/0.4 weighted), 62-dim features: expanded to 9 physicochemical props (added flexibility, helix/sheet propensity, ASA), local ±4 residue context, binary charge-change and Pro/Gly flags.
rounds_summary.observation: Exit 0, 147 rows saved. MAE=66.27°C — switching to tree ensembles and adding structural propensity features gave no improvement; MAE regressed slightly vs round 2.

ruled_out_routes: sklearn MLPRegressor with hidden layers (256,128,64) or (512,256,128,64) + StandardScaler + Adam + early stopping on hand-crafted tabular features (MAE ~67°C).
sklearn GradientBoostingRegressor (n_estimators=500, lr=0.05, max_depth=4) + RandomForest ensemble on physicochemical differential features (MAE ~66°C).
20-dim amino acid composition of full mutant/WT sequence as primary feature block — demonstrated noisy and non-informative for deltaTm prediction across two rounds.
Hand-crafted local context window (±4 residues, averaged 9 physicochemical props) as structural environment proxy — did not recover meaningful signal in round 3.
synthesised_diagnosis: Across all three rounds the agent kept running into a representation ceiling: every approach relied exclusively on per-residue physicochemical lookup tables and sequence composition, which encode only amino acid identity, not 3D structural context. The mutation site's burial depth, hydrogen-bond network, and backbone strain — the primary determinants of deltaTm — are invisible to these features. No model family (MLP, GBR, RF) or feature enrichment within this hand-crafted tabular paradigm can recover that information, so MAE is locked near ~65°C regardless of architecture.
rationale: All three model families converged to MAE ~65°C with no improvement trajectory, confirming the failure is not architectural but representational: hand-crafted physicochemical features cannot encode the 3D structural determinants of thermal stability, making further tuning within this paradigm futile.
recommended_alternative: Use the ESM-2 protein language model (Meta, available via `fair-esm` pip package) to extract per-residue embeddings for both WT and mutant sequences. Compute the difference embedding at the mutation position (esm2_mut[resn] - esm2_wt[resn], 1280-dim for esm2_t33_650M_UR50D), concatenate with Tmexp_wt scalar, then fit a Ridge or LightGBM regressor. ESM-2 encodes learned structural context from 250M proteins and has been shown to correlate with stability in prior benchmarks.
